_doc: Deterministic post-LLM HCL corrections. See importer/post_llm_overrides.py for the rule shape, when to add entries, and the failure model. Each entry MUST include a `comment` field explaining WHY the rename/deletion is correct -- future maintainers should be able to verify the fix is still valid against the current provider schema without git-archaeology.

renames.comment: The reservation_affinity block's discriminator field is named `type` in the current provider schema (registry.terraform.io/hashicorp/google v6+). The LLM frequently emits `consume_reservation_type` instead, which is the underlying GCP API field name -- it appears that way in older provider docs and in the raw cloud JSON, but Terraform's HCL surface uses the shorter `type`. Mechanical rename, always correct within reservation_affinity{} blocks. Verified: importer/knowledge_base/google_compute_instance.json shows reservation_affinity.type as the correct path; consume_reservation_type does not exist in the schema.

P2-2: the cloud snapshot has `nodeLocations` (camelCase API field name); the LLM frequently emits `locations = [...]` at resource-body root, which the provider rejects ('argument named locations is not expected here'). Correct schema field is `node_locations` (snake_case). Universally correct rename for both Standard zonal and Standard regional clusters. Autopilot clusters are protected separately by resource_mode.py's gke_autopilot prune list (which strips `nodeLocations` from the snapshot before the LLM sees it, since Autopilot manages node placement and rejects manual `node_locations` config). block_path empty -> dispatches to _rename_at_top_level. Surfaced by the Phase 1 SMOKE on poc-cluster-std (zonal Standard cluster).

deletions.comment: P2-8: v1 schema vestige. google_cloud_run_service (v1) had `container_concurrency` on `template.spec.container_concurrency`. v2 renamed and relocated this to `template.max_instance_request_concurrency` with a different semantic. The LLM, trained on v1 docs, frequently emits the legacy name on a v2 resource. Provider rejects with 'argument named container_concurrency is not expected here'. Field has NO valid placement in the v2 schema -> safe to delete from anywhere (block_path empty -> _delete_at_top_level). Surfaced by the Phase 2 SMOKE on poc-cloudrun.

P2-8: v1 schema vestige. v1's `traffic` block accepted `latest_revision = true` to direct 100% of traffic to the latest revision. v2 redesigned the traffic block: instead of a boolean, it uses `type = "TRAFFIC_TARGET_ALLOCATION_TYPE_LATEST"`. The LLM emits the v1 boolean on v2 resources; provider rejects. Field has NO valid placement in v2 schema -> delete from anywhere. Surfaced by the Phase 2 SMOKE on poc-cloudrun.

P4-11 (re-surfaced in SMOKE 4 on poc-cloudrun): `startup_cpu_boost` was a top-level template field in v1; v2 relocated the concept under `template.containers[].startup_probe` with a different shape. The cloud snapshot's `template.startupCpuBoost` survives gcloud describe and the LLM echoes it back. Provider rejects: 'argument named startup_cpu_boost is not expected here'. Same v1-vestige class as the two siblings above (P2-8, P2-12). The CC-9 golden example pointed at the right SHAPE but the LLM still emitted the field because the input JSON had it -- few-shot didn't override input data. Three-layer fix: snapshot_scrubber strips it pre-LLM (defense layer 1, see snapshot_scrubber.py), this entry strips it post-LLM (defense layer 2), golden example trimmed to remove the misleading 'NO startup_cpu_boost' comment (defense layer 3 -- absence is the signal, not prose).
